title: What's the perfect encoding? How do you know?
summary: 3Blue1Brown examines the problem of defining and achieving perfect encoding, exploring what metrics and constraints matter when evaluating encoding schemes. The video questions how we can know whether an encoding is optimal for a given purpose.
writeup: 3Blue1Brown takes up the question of what "perfect encoding" means and how its optimality can be established, framing the problem through information theory. The video lays out the metrics and constraints that determine whether an encoding scheme is good for a given purpose. The central point is that judging optimality depends on defining the right criteria first, since "perfect" is meaningful only relative to a stated goal.
